metadata.title:
Plenty OF COAL


BUT FEW CARS

pages.raw_text:
: Wyoming mines shipping 125
cars Of coal east daily.


Same mines could ship so car
loads west daily-but they can't
get the cars.


Only STRAY" cars on the Union
Pacific are loaded for western ship
ment.


Investigation of Spokane dealer
bears out contention that country
is suffering results of frenzied
finance In railroading.


These are the conclusions of Mel
son Martin, manager of the Rock
Springs Coal & Wood Co., who re.
turned from a trip to the Wyom-
ing coal mines last night. Mr..
Martin said the Wyoming coal
fields are loading 1875 cars daily,
but all for eastern markets. He is
convinced that the prevailing short.
age of coal in this country is due
entirely to lack of cars and that
Tie ChOltS VI I1lll dIu nal iIiidli
to initiate dividends is responsible
for this scarcity of rolling stock.
The roads will not spend money
enough for sufficient new equip.
ment.


All the coal that is shipped west
from the Wyoming mines comes in
cars that belong to other roads,
"strays" they are called. If a
Northern Pacific car happens into
the region 1t is loaded with coal
and sent west, but none Of the
cars belonging to the Union Pa.
ctic are used for shipping coal
west. Mr.. Martin saId that they
were loading five cars at the Mon.
arch mines when he was there,
and that he secured all the cars
for Spokane. He also said that
